title: Uplift Agent Studio playbook definition
description: A playbook is a named, declarative definition: a trigger, a roster of owned agents (each optionally narrowed to a tool subset), an autonomy level, and the Greenlight policy. SPEC, NOT CODE — the definition transmits data only; nothing in it is executable, and side-effecting tools can never be granted autonomy here (greenlight.side_effects is the constant 'always_ask': every send/CRM write routes through Greenlight, draft-only).

description.description: What this playbook does, for the Studio library card.

kind.description: manual = run on demand; schedule = cron expression; event = a named domain event.

schedule.description: Cron expression (UTC). Required when kind is 'schedule'.

event.description: Domain event name (e.g. lead.created). Required when kind is 'event'.

roster.description: The owned specialists this playbook runs. Code-level validation additionally requires each agent to exist in the owned roster and each tool to be a subset of that agent's owned tool grant (no privilege escalation via JSON).

tools.description: Optional narrowing: a subset of the agent's owned tools. Omitted = the agent's full owned grant.

autonomy.description: Autonomy dial level (api/control/autonomy.py). Read-only tools auto-run at any level; side-effecting tools ALWAYS route through Greenlight regardless (see greenlight.side_effects).

side_effects.description: DRAFT-ONLY, enforced at the schema level: the only legal value is 'always_ask'. A playbook can never grant a send/CRM write autonomy.

note.description: Optional reviewer note shown alongside Greenlight proposals from this playbook.
